rankings.name: No Drama This End
key_positive_factors: Unbeaten in three hurdle starts including Grade 1 Challow Novices' Hurdle
Highest RPR (156) in the field
Top British hope for leading trainer Paul Nicholls
Proven stamina for 2m5f trip
key_risks: First appearance at Cheltenham Festival
Significant challenge from strong Irish contingent

rankings.name: King Rasko Grey
key_positive_factors: Paul Townend's choice from six Willie Mullins runners
Close third in Leopardstown Grade 1 behind Ballyfad
Sire (Galiway) and yard both have excellent Festival records
Consistent profile (4-213)
key_risks: Beaten twice in Grade 1 company already
Needs to find a few pounds to reverse form with Ballyfad

rankings.name: Skylight Hustle
key_positive_factors: Highest Top Speed (148) in the field
Grade 1 winner at Leopardstown in December
Strong upward trajectory for Gordon Elliott
Wide-margin win at Fairyhouse showed elite potential
key_risks: Pedigree asks a slight question of the 2m5f trip
Jockey booking (Danny Gilligan) suggests he may be Elliott second-string

rankings.name: Ballyfad
key_positive_factors: Runner-up in top-tier Leopardstown Grade 1 on last start
Jack Kennedy remains on board for Gordon Elliott
Solid OR 149 and high RPR 151
Unbeaten in points and bumpers prior to hurdles
key_risks: Claimed on the line last time; may lack a change of gear at the finish
Trip is new, though pedigree is encouraging

rankings.name: Ill Sort That
key_positive_factors: Unbeaten in four hurdle starts including Naas Grade 1
Strong TS 138 and RPR 151 indicate high-level capability
Gritty attitude shown under pressure last time
Forcing tactics could be effective in this field
key_risks: May find it harder to dominate a larger, higher-quality field
Yard is smaller compared to Mullins/Elliott giants

rankings.name: Sober
key_positive_factors: Group class Flat performer (Queen Alexandra winner at Royal Ascot)
Unbeaten in two starts over hurdles including Grade 2 win
Highly progressive profile for Willie Mullins
Patrick Mullins takes the ride
key_risks: Hurdling technique needs to tighten up for Grade 1 pressure
Late to the game in this sphere despite Flat ability

rankings.name: Act Of Innocence
key_positive_factors: Won Huntingdon Listed event comfortably on latest outing
Progressive RPR (146) and solid TS (129)
Nicky Henderson runner with Nico de Boinville on board
Follows the Shishkin path to the Festival
key_risks: Surprisingly beaten in a Newbury maiden in December
Lower OR (138) than the primary Irish contenders

rankings.name: Sortudo
key_positive_factors: Runner-up to Ill Sort That in Naas Grade 1 last time
Consistent performer for Willie Mullins
High TS 135 suggests plenty of speed for the finish
Useful bumper horse with course experience
key_risks: Needs to reverse form with Ill Sort That
Seems to be a secondary choice for the yard

rankings.name: Bossman Jack
key_positive_factors: Improving winner of last two hurdle starts
Representing last year's winning yard (Dan Skelton)
High TS (137) and improving RPR (149)
Dam won over this trip
key_risks: Lowest official rating (134) of the rated contenders
Step up to Grade 1 is a significant jump in class

rankings.name: Shuttle Diplomacy
key_positive_factors: 3rd in last year's Champion Bumper at this Festival
Resurgent since fitting of tongue-tie, winning at Naas
Beaten King Rasko Grey in points and ran well against him over hurdles
key_risks: Form can be inconsistent (5-P7)
Stamina for 2m5f trip is not yet proven

rankings.name: Zeus Power
key_positive_factors: Won last two starts in impressive fashion
Step back up in trip likely to suit for Joseph O'Brien
Improving RPR (145) profile
key_risks: Beaten earlier this season by Riskaway
Faces significantly deeper quality here

rankings.name: Hurricane Pat
key_positive_factors: Dual hurdle winner earlier this season
Strong TS (135) for Gary & Josh Moore yard
Step up in trip could unlock further improvement
key_risks: Limitations seemingly exposed when soundly beaten at Haydock last time
Needs a career best to figure at this level

rankings.name: Walks In June
key_positive_factors: Handicap winner at Leopardstown on latest outing
Yard (De Bromhead) runners always command respect at Cheltenham
key_risks: Seemingly exposed in Grade 3 company at Navan
Lowest TS (96) of the runners with speed data

rankings.name: Laurets d'Estruval
key_positive_factors: Mullins entry who won easily at Thurles
Unknown ceiling after just three starts
key_risks: Paul Townend chose other stable options
Lacks the experience and RPR profile of principals

rankings.name: Taurus Bay
key_positive_factors: Just denied in Grade 2 here in January
Plucky attitude and proven track form
key_risks: Form (RPR 139) leaves him with significant work to do against G1 winners

rankings.name: Saint Baco
key_positive_factors: Impressive debut win for Mullins at Navan
key_risks: Dismal run in Naas Grade 1 last time
Townend and market signals are negative

rankings.name: Klimt Madrik
key_positive_factors: Second to No Drama This End in the Challow G1
Solid, honest attitude in his races
key_risks: Beaten 10 lengths by No Drama This End last time; hard to see the reversal

rankings.name: Soldier Reeves
key_positive_factors: Winning debut at Cheltenham in November
key_risks: Readily beaten by Hurricane Pat and others since
Needs massive step up

rankings.name: Riskaway
key_positive_factors: Consistent Grade 1 performer (4th at Leopardstown)
key_risks: Form profile suggests he is a cut below the leading players

rankings.name: Fortune Timmy
key_positive_factors: Third in a Grade 2 here in January
key_risks: Lowest RPR (137) in the field
Likely outclassed

rankings.name: Free Spirit
key_positive_factors: Representing top connections (Roche/Walsh/JP McManus)
key_risks: Market and form indicators suggest low probability today

forecast_top_3.combination: No Drama This End > King Rasko Grey
No Drama This End > Skylight Hustle
King Rasko Grey > No Drama This End

tricast_top_3.combination: No Drama This End > King Rasko Grey > Skylight Hustle
No Drama This End > Skylight Hustle > King Rasko Grey
King Rasko Grey > No Drama This End > Skylight Hustle

pace_analysis.insight: The forcing tactics of Ill Sort That will ensure a true test of stamina, playing directly into the hands of the Challow winner No Drama This End.
